title: Verascore POST /api/publish — concordia-receipt envelope (accepted contract)
description: Committed copy of the body Verascore's /api/publish route accepts for a concordia-receipt. Bound to Verascore src/app/api/publish/route.ts and src/lib/concordia-receipts.ts on branch at commit a3e6090 (#63, 'require counterparty co-signature for trust-bearing Concordia receipts'). The route destructures {signature, publicKey, type, data}; agentId is NOT trusted (derived from the verified publicKey via deriveAgentId). The publisher-envelope signature is raw Ed25519 over JSON.stringify(data), base64url-encoded (route.ts: base64urlToBuffer + verifyEd25519). data.receipt is extracted (extractConcordiaReceiptPayload) and must carry session_id, counterparty_did, outcome, and concordia (protocol version); parties[] carries the counterparty co-signature (concordia-cosignature.ts).

publicKey.description: base64url raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key; route requires length 32 after decode.

signature.description: base64url raw 64-byte Ed25519 signature over JSON.stringify(data); route requires length 64 after decode.

did.description: Publisher did:key; route rejects (403) if it does not match publicKey (publicKeyMatchesDid).

concordia.description: Protocol version; normalizeConcordiaReceipt reads receipt.concordia and rejects when absent.

signature.description: base64url Ed25519 counterparty co-signature; present only on the counterparty_did party (findCounterpartySignature).
